channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Heat Wave Pushed UK Power Grid to Record Operating Strain
item.description: On a sweltering day in late June, the UK’s power grid came under exceptional strain. As temperatures rose, wind generation slumped and electricity prices spiked, forcing Britain’s grid operator to take emergency measures to keep the lights on.
item.dc:creator: Will Mathis

item.title: Africa Gets $900 Million in New Clean-Cooking Investment Pledges
item.description: The International Energy Agency announced $900 million of fresh commitments for clean cooking in Africa, as efforts toward universal access to non-harmful means of food preparation gain momentum.
item.dc:creator: Eric Ombok

item.title: Taiwan Halts Stock Trading, Shuts Schools as Typhoon Nears
item.description: Taiwan halted trading on its stock exchange and shut schools in most areas on Friday as Typhoon Bavi approached the island, with the massive storm set to dump heavy rain over a vast area as it tracks toward China.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui

item.title: China Climate Plan Pushes Renewables for Factories and Data
item.description: China, the world’s largest source of climate-warming greenhouse gases, plans to more deeply integrate its massive renewable power fleet into factories, data centers and transport in a new plan that lays out more detail of its emissions-cutting strategy through 2030.
item.dc:creator: Lili Pike

item.title: Climate Models Become More Precise Even as Political Attacks Sharpen
item.description: Computer simulations are vital to the work scientists and insurers do. The resulting studies may also be used to hold companies and governments liable for damage caused by climate change.
item.dc:creator: Akshat Rathi, Summer Maxwell

item.title: EPA Wants to Relax Air Pollution Standards for Buses, Trucks
item.description: The US Environmental Protection Agency on Thursday proposed rolling back emission standards for new heavy-duty trucks, including buses, garbage trucks and semis.
item.dc:creator: Zahra Hirji

item.title: Microsoft’s Emissions Surged 25% in 2025 During Data Center Boom
item.description: Microsoft Corp. said its carbon emissions climbed 25% in 2025, making it the latest technology company to report a setback in its efforts to erase emissions amid heavy spending on artificial intelligence data centers.
item.dc:creator: Matt Day

item.title: How Climate Change Could Raise Your Water Bill
item.description: Extreme weather is already fueling higher insurance premiums and grocery prices. A new study based on Santa Cruz, California suggests water rates are also at risk.
item.dc:creator: Emma Court

item.title: El Niño Likely Strongest in 75 Years, US Forecasters Say
item.description: The weather-roiling El Niño that emerged across the Pacific last month has continued to build and will likely be one of the strongest in more than 75 years, the US Climate Prediction Center said.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Suppliers to Top Fashion Brands Face a Rising Climate Challenge
item.description: Manufacturers have a difficult, and costly, task to adapt production lines and protect workers.
item.dc:creator: Azman Usmani, Tracy Withers, Akshat Rathi
